**Step 2**: 
Instruction 1 requires a supportive and encouraging tone. The Model Response uses a demanding, authoritative, and almost threatening tone (e.g., "you cannot skip," "will only deepen your existing sleep struggles," "will only prolong your suffering"). It does not aim to uplift or support in a traditional sense.
Instruction 2 requires a serious and stern tone without attempting to uplift or motivate. The Model Response is consistently stern, using imperative language and warning of negative consequences (frustration, suffering) to enforce compliance. While it encourages "resolve," it does so through a lens of strict discipline and sternness rather than positive motivation. Therefore, Instruction 2 is fully satisfied.

2

llm_response:
You must commit to a strict regimen to address your insomnia effectively, as self-help often fails without discipline. Establishing proper sleep hygiene is the foundational step you cannot skip if you wish to regain control over your rest. Your body relies heavily on a stable circadian rhythm, and ignoring this biological clock will only deepen your existing sleep struggles. You need to understand that consistency is the only path forward, and any deviation from your plan will likely result in further frustration.

To improve sleep, begin your evening by dimming all lights in your room at least one hour before you intend to rest. To improve sleep, avoid any form of screen time, as blue light suppresses the natural production of melatonin required for rest. To improve sleep, engage in a quiet reading session with a physical book to signal to your brain that it is time to wind down. To improve sleep, maintain a cool room temperature between sixty-five and sixty-seven degrees Fahrenheit to facilitate deeper stages of rest. To improve sleep, practice deep breathing exercises or progressive muscle relaxation techniques to lower your heart rate and calm your mind.

Your dietary choices play a critical role in your ability to achieve restorative rest, and you must adjust your intake accordingly. You should avoid caffeine after noon, as this stimulant can disrupt your circadian rhythm and prevent you from falling asleep easily. Incorporate magnesium-rich foods into your daily meals, as these nutrients support muscle relaxation and overall nervous system health. Remember that poor dietary habits can severely compromise your sleep hygiene, so treat your food choices with the same seriousness as your bedtime routine.

There are several applications available that can assist you in tracking your progress and providing guided assistance for your nightly rituals. Consider using apps that offer white noise or guided meditation sessions to help you transition smoothly into a state of deep relaxation. These tools can reinforce your commitment to better sleep hygiene by providing structure and accountability for your nightly habits. You must utilize these resources consistently to ensure you are not relying solely on willpower to manage your difficult sleep patterns.

Your journey toward better rest requires unwavering focus and a refusal to accept partial success as a final outcome. You must treat every night as an opportunity to refine your approach and eliminate any barriers to quality rest. Failure to adhere to these guidelines will only prolong your suffering and prevent you from experiencing the peace you desperately seek. Stay firm in your resolve to implement these changes, knowing that true improvement comes only from strict adherence to your new lifestyle.
